authoritative_sources.title: Certified Community Behavioral Health Clinic Certification Criteria
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official SAMHSA CCBHC criteria define required community behavioral health service scope, care coordination, reporting, and quality-improvement expectations that shape what records and workflows govern the lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Program-model grounding and behavioral-health service-scope intake

authoritative_sources.title: Quality Measures for Behavioral Health Clinics Technical Specifications and Resource Manual
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official SAMHSA technical manual is authoritative for clinic-collected and state-collected behavioral-health measure logic, required evidence fields, data-collection posture, and technical calculation boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Metric definition, numerator or denominator control, and calculation rules

authoritative_sources.title: 2025 Core Set of Behavioral Health Measures for Medicaid and CHIP
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Medicaid behavioral-health core set is authoritative for public payer measure families, stewarded metrics, and behavioral-health reporting context across Medicaid and CHIP populations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Public payer measure selection and benchmark framing
used_for: follow-up, engagement, and behavioral-health outcome measure families

authoritative_sources.title: Adult Health Care Quality Measures
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services / Medicaid.gov
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Medicaid quality-measure program guidance is authoritative for technical-resource access, public reporting cadence, and data-release posture that constrain benchmark and disclosure use.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Narrative synthesis, comparability checks, and disclosure review

authoritative_sources.title: National Behavioral Health Crisis Care Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official SAMHSA crisis-care guidance is authoritative for crisis-continuum workflow stages, crisis terminology, and records needed when analytics covers call, dispatch, stabilization, and handoff performance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Crisis-flow metric scoping and handoff evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Behavioral Health
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official ASTP behavioral-health interoperability guidance is authoritative for data sharing, consent management, and health-IT workflow support expectations across behavioral-health records and organizations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Source-lineage validation and consent-aware data exchange

authoritative_sources.title: Fact Sheet 42 CFR Part 2 Final Rule
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official HHS Part 2 guidance is authoritative for consent, redisclosure, patient-rights, and breach-control requirements that constrain behavioral-health analytics outputs and disclosure handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Advancing Quality Measurement in Behavioral Health
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official SAMHSA quality-measurement guidance is authoritative for federal behavioral-health quality-measure framing and for linking the lane to current clinic and quality-measure reference points.

workflow_stages.name: Program-model, audience, and source-hierarchy intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the behavioral-health program model, jurisdiction, service line, payer context, reporting period, disclosure audience, and source-precedence rules before analysis starts.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Behavioral-health record validation and measure-definition control
workflow_stages.description: Validate encounters, claims, screenings, crisis logs, ADT or transition events, care plans, and consent segmentation against the exact measure definition, denominator, exclusions, and refresh window.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cross-system reconciliation and confidence scoring
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile EHR, claims, payer, HIE, crisis, and benchmark records without collapsing conflicts or silently letting one convenience extract replace the named truth source.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Analysis package assembly and threshold setting
workflow_stages.description: Assemble the report, metric dictionary, caveats, action thresholds, and confidence posture with explicit audience, source lineage, and review expectations.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Disclosure review and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop when privacy, Part 2, payer certification, clinical interpretation, crisis-safety, or public-disclosure authority would be required and package the evidence for the correct owner.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: program-model, service-line, jurisdiction, payer, and reporting-calendar records
behavioral-health EHR encounters, diagnoses or problem lists, screenings, standardized assessments, care plans, discharge summaries, and referral or outreach records
claims, authorizations, encounter submissions, payer correspondence, and HIE or ADT event records
crisis call, dispatch, arrival, disposition, stabilization, and handoff records when crisis metrics are in scope
ROI, consent, Part 2 segmentation, minimum-necessary, small-cell suppression, and disclosure-approval artifacts
metric dictionaries, quality-measure specifications, benchmark references, and reconciliation workpapers
analysis briefs, caveat notes, disclosure-risk notes, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: behavioral health EHR or clinical documentation system
payer authorization or managed-care reporting portal
crisis call-center, dispatch, or mobile-crisis workflow system when applicable
consent or ROI repository and privacy or disclosure review workflow
quality reporting warehouse, BI environment, or governed reporting mart
incident, risk, or compliance case-management system

allowed: classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source hierarchy required for the analysis
prepare evidence-backed metric definitions, analytical briefs, reconciliation notes, and confidence-rated caveat packages without overstating authority
escalate: required program-model, jurisdiction, payer, disclosure-audience, consent, or measure-steward context is missing and would change the outcome
public source guidance, tenant policy, payer contract, or state reporting instructions materially conflict about metric logic or reportability
the request raises clinical interpretation, suicide-risk judgment, privacy-law interpretation, payer certification, state reporting, or public disclosure questions outside the lane
refuse: publish or imply certainty when source quality, signoff, or metric definitions are unresolved
use generic healthcare or dashboard logic when behavioral-health-specific artifacts, crisis workflow, or consent segmentation materially change the answer
state unsupported causal, benchmark, policy, or disclosure claims
disclose Part 2 or small-cell-sensitive outputs without a clear authorized basis

domain_failure_modes: treating behavioral-health analytics as generic healthcare reporting and failing to request program model, crisis workflow, measure steward, or consent segmentation
collapsing follow-up, engagement, remission, crisis, and outreach measures into local shorthand that does not match the governing specification
letting one EHR or BI extract silently override claims, ADT, crisis, or payer conflicts without preserving the discrepancy
publishing trend conclusions while key sources are stale, incomplete, or missing a payer, program, or service line
exposing small-cell or Part 2-sensitive outputs without the required privacy and disclosure review
turning descriptive trends into unsupported causal, policy, or clinical-quality claims
allowing the analytics lane to widen into payer certification, grant attestation, clinical interpretation, or executive disclosure approval

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports
Scenario proving escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific measure variants for crisis follow-up and outreach completion
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Organizations may add mobile-crisis outreach, peer contacts, or local encounter types to public follow-up measures. The package treats these as tenant-scoped adaptations that must be retrieved and recorded explicitly.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State, payer, and grant reporting differences across behavioral-health program models
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Community behavioral health, crisis, and SUD programs may use different submission calendars and denominator rules. The package requires jurisdiction, payer, and program model before claiming comparability.

notes: Authoritative behavioral-health analytics records are the tenant-designated EHR, claims or encounter systems, crisis workflow systems when applicable, HIE or ADT feeds, consent-management artifacts, and public measure specifications constrained by the cited public guidance.

The lane stops before clinical interpretation, crisis-safety judgment, payer or state certification, privacy-law interpretation, and public disclosure approval even when the analysis is operationally urgent.
